channel.title: bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Mechanical forces from the beating heart may help prevent cancer cell growth
item.description: Scientists may have discovered another way the human body tries to protect itself from cancer. New research on mice suggests that the heart's constant...

item.title: Sweet discovery rewrites understanding of how our bodies store sugar
item.description: WEHI researchers have discovered a never-before-seen mechanism our bodies use to regulate sugar, in findings that rewrite the fundamental rules of bio...

item.title: Study finds children in the US die at higher rates than peers in other high-income nations
item.description: At every age, children in the United States die at higher rates than peers in other high-income nations, according to a new study from Children's Hosp...

item.title: Alcohol causes more cancers in Australia than previously thought
item.description: Alcohol consumption is likely to cause more cancer incidence in Australia than previously thought, according to new research from the University of Sy...

item.title: Improving cardiovascular risk prediction in Latin America and the Caribbean: SCORE2-LAC
item.description: A preliminary model to improve the prediction of cardiovascular risk in Latin America and the Caribbean was presented at ESC Preventive Cardiology 202...

item.title: RHR: The GLP-1 Blind Spot: What Ozempic Won’t Do for Your Metabolic Health
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris explores the science behind GLP-1 medications, including their powerful effects on appetite, weight ...

item.title: RHR: Shaking Up the Salt Myth
item.description: For decades, public health guidelines have warned that reducing sodium intake is essential for preventing high blood pressure and cardiovascular disea...

item.title: RHR: Mouth Taping: Beyond the Hype—What the Science Really Says
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explores the viral trend of mouth taping and explains why the reality is more complex than t...

item.title: RHR: Erythritol: The ‘Safe’ Sweetener That’s Anything But
item.description: Erythritol, a popular sweetener found in countless sugar-free and keto products, has long been considered a safe alternative to sugar. This episode of...

item.title: RHR: Preserving Cognitive Function As You Age, with Tommy Wood
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser is joined by neuroscientist and physician Dr. Tommy Wood to explore how cognitive function c...
